subtopics.title: Meaning and Objectives of Accounting
common_misconceptions: Accounting is just mathematics
Accounting and bookkeeping are identical
subtopics.teaching_note: Distinguish between bookkeeping and accounting. Discuss the various users of accounting information and their needs.

subtopics.title: Principles and Concepts of Accounting
common_misconceptions: Accounting principles are laws
Rules never change across organizations
subtopics.teaching_note: Explain each concept with practical examples. Emphasize that these are guidelines, not rigid laws.

topics.title: Source Documents and Books of Original Entry

subtopics.title: Source Documents
common_misconceptions: All source documents are the same
An invoice is proof of payment
subtopics.teaching_note: Show real or sample source documents. Explain the purpose and content of each document.

subtopics.title: Books of Original Entry
common_misconceptions: Cash book is part of the ledger
All transactions go through the general journal first
subtopics.teaching_note: Demonstrate how transactions flow from source documents to books of original entry. Practice with sample transactions.

topics.title: Ledger Accounts and the Trial Balance

subtopics.title: The Ledger and Posting
common_misconceptions: Debit always means decrease
Credit always means increase
subtopics.teaching_note: Teach the golden rules of accounting. Emphasize that debit/credit meaning depends on account type.

subtopics.title: Trial Balance
concepts: errors not disclosed by trial balance
common_misconceptions: A balanced trial balance means no errors
Trial balance is a financial statement
subtopics.teaching_note: Explain that a trial balance checks arithmetic accuracy only. Discuss errors that don't affect trial balance agreement.

subtopics.title: Trading Account
common_misconceptions: Sales minus purchases equals gross profit
Opening stock is not part of cost of goods sold
subtopics.teaching_note: Derive the cost of goods sold formula. Show the step-by-step preparation of a trading account.

subtopics.title: Profit and Loss Account
common_misconceptions: Net profit is the same as cash in hand
All expenses appear in the trading account
subtopics.teaching_note: Distinguish between direct expenses (trading account) and indirect expenses (P&L account). Practice with comprehensive examples.

subtopics.title: Balance Sheet
common_misconceptions: Balance sheet shows profit or loss
Assets are always equal to capital
subtopics.teaching_note: Emphasize that balance sheet shows financial position at a point in time. Distinguish from income statement which covers a period.

subtopics.title: Types of Cash Book
common_misconceptions: Trade discount is recorded in cash book
Contra entries don't need double entry
subtopics.teaching_note: Show the progression from simple to complex cash books. Emphasize the treatment of contra entries.

subtopics.title: Bank Reconciliation Statement
common_misconceptions: Bank reconciliation corrects bank errors only
The adjusted balance must match the bank statement
subtopics.teaching_note: Explain timing differences first. Then deal with errors. Show the step-by-step reconciliation process.
